scenario: Hard lockup due to data race with out-of-tree fl2000 module
symptoms: WARNING: CPU: 4 PID: 9179 at include/linux/kref.h:52 fl2000_device_probe+0x304/0x3e0 [fl2000]
NMI watchdog: Watchdog detected hard LOCKUP on cpu 4
Kernel panic - not syncing: Hard LOCKUP

workflow.observation: Memory dump showing spinlock val at 0xffff921a2b8b88b0+8 = 0xffff921a00218928

workflow.thought: Check kernel memory for the suspicious address
workflow.observation: ZONE: Normal, AREA: 4096k, PAGE: ffffd8c145008600, PHYSICAL: 140218000

root_cause_analysis: A hard lockup is defined as a bug that causes the CPU to loop in kernel mode without giving other tasks a chance to run for more than 10 seconds.
In this case, the spinlock is not actually a spinlock but overwritten illegally due to some possible data race that is possibly caused by fl2000
